steps.title: Check the given points
steps.content: The relationship must pass through $(0,45)$, $(6,81)$, and $(15,0)$. A matching quadratic has to produce all three outputs.

steps.title: Test the matching quadratic
steps.content: For $y=-x^2+12x+45$, substitute the two nonzero $x$-values: $$-(6)^2+12(6)+45=-36+72+45=81$$ $$-(15)^2+12(15)+45=-225+180+45=0$$ It also gives $45$ when $x=0$ because the constant term is $45$. The answer is B.
